1 First Load — Chat Interface
interest Confidence 0.60

"Okay, so this drops me right into a chat interface — no landing page, no product tour. Bold choice. I see GPT-5.4 as the default model, that's current. There's a 'Buy a license' badge in the top right... and some onboarding checklist hiding in the sidebar. I don't need that — let me find pricing first."

⚠ No product overview or pricing upfront on first load
2 Pricing Page Review
interest Confidence 0.75

"There we go — three tiers, all lifetime. Standard at $39, Extended at $79, Premium at $99 which is 50% off from $198. Lifetime license, buy once use forever — that's a solid value prop compared to $20/mo for ChatGPT Plus. Premium gets me multi-model chats, unlimited plugins, projects & folders. That's what I need."

✓ Pricing is transparent and competitive
3 Plan Feature Comparison
trust Confidence 0.80

"Nice, they have a detailed comparison docs site. Standard/Extended/Premium feature matrix. Chat features, knowledge base, speech, plugins, models... all three plans support GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Grok, Mistral, OpenRouter. The main difference is plugins and advanced features are Premium-only. That confirms it — Premium is the only plan that makes sense for me."

✓ Feature comparison docs are well-organized
4 TypingMind vs ChatGPT Plus
admiration Confidence 0.85

"This is exactly what I wanted. Head-to-head comparison with ChatGPT Plus. TypingMind has green checks across the board — Claude, Gemini, Mistral, OpenRouter, Azure, Ollama, Perplexity... ChatGPT Plus is red X on all non-GPT models. That's the whole point — I need multi-model access in one interface."

✓ Multi-model support clearly surpasses ChatGPT Plus
5 Pricing & Privacy Features
anticipation Confidence 0.88

"Scrolling down... token count and cost estimation — that's huge, ChatGPT Plus doesn't show you that. Native RAG support, only TypingMind has it. Plugins are more flexible here. Privacy section — custom proxy, API key protection, cloud sync. Pricing row confirms it: one-time purchase starting at $39 vs ChatGPT Plus monthly from $20."

⚠ Wide comparison table may cause horizontal scroll on mobile
6 BYOK API Key Management
trust Confidence 0.90

"Okay, the API key setup. Clean — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, each with a direct link to get your key. 'Your API Key is stored locally on your browser and never sent anywhere else.' Good, that's the right approach for BYOK. This is exactly what I expected. I can plug in my existing OpenAI and Anthropic keys and be up and running in 30 seconds."

✓ BYOK API key setup is fast and secure
7 Model Selector
joy Confidence 0.92

"Model selector — GPT-5.4 with 1M context window, that's the latest. GPT-5.4 Mini and Nano marked as NEW. Claude Sonnet 4.6 with 200K, Gemini 3.1 Pro with 1M. Plus custom models for Llama, Mistral, Perplexity. This is exactly what a power user needs — switch between models with one click. No logging in to different services, no separate browser tabs."

✓ Seamless multi-model switching, no friction
8 FAQ — ChatGPT Plus vs API Cost
annoyance Confidence 0.88

"FAQ says ChatGPT Plus and API offer the same model and quality. Cost comparison: "Typical chat conversation costs ~$0.001, less than a cent." Hmm, that's a bit misleading for power users like me. I'm sending large context windows for code review — my actual spend is $55/mo, not $1. But still, $55/mo API + $99 one-time license vs $20/mo ChatGPT Plus which caps usage? Easy math."

⚠ FAQ cost claims misleading for power users

admiration Confidence 0.93

"Settings panel — this is well organized. App Data & Storage with Import From OpenAI? That's killer for migration. Cloud Sync, API Keys, License Key management. Model Context Protocol support — they're keeping up with the ecosystem. Storage stats show everything is local in browser with IndexedDB. This is a serious tool, not a toy."

✓ Settings panel is comprehensive and well-organized
10 Final Interface Review
optimism Confidence 0.95

"Back to the main chat. Clean interface — model selector, @ mentions for AI agents, knowledge base toggle, thinking mode, background mode. Overall, this is what ChatGPT should have been.
